reviews.review_msg: The final exam is very difficult. many unexpected questions compared to a relatively easy midterm. you need to go to his class in order to earn attendance mark; no homework solution would be given by TA or himself; I don't recommend him at all
Very bad
HARD EXAM! DOESNT HELP! NO USE!

Has an attitude problem and never answers a simple e-mail. Had to go to the Mathematics director to get a response from him. He's a decent teacher but has poor organization skills. His marking breakdown didn't match  the one the website. He said at the start of semester that we could miss one homework and one quiz except he counted all of them.

Test were not bad, but he didn't scale as promised, and changed the marking scheme on us without telling anyone. Appears easy going in lecture, but is very difficult to deal with in person. Large chunk of mark was attendence, but he himself was away for several weeks. You can do well on tests and still get a bad grade.
Hard final exam compared to his midterm. Assignments require a lot of work. I needed a 0.25% to get a B+ and he REFUSED to bump me up! So he is very uncooperative and a bit annoying.
He didn't post solutions for the midterm or assignments. His final was not easy at all
Before I took this class I did not fully understand linear Algebra but the methods he showed us, were very clear and easy to understand.

Overall the course was fair, its more of a plain math course where you learn an algo. and you apply it. However, final seemed rather complex compared to midterm and homework. He is a good prof. giving examples for the algo. he teaches so its definitely more helpful than other profs that may not do that and expect u to learn it yourself.
Hard final exam :(

Dr. Oberman definitely is a great prof. he never complicates things and explains everything thoughfully during class. attendence quizzes + quizzes help us alot. perhaps the homework takes a bit long to complete but it is worth doing them coz you would learn. be sure to attend class all the time. it is surprised that he won lots of awards this year.
The course is tough, but he is good and tries to make it easy. He is clear, helpful and cracks jokes during lectures out of nowhere to keep people interested. All in all, a really good prof.

Really strives to help students understand the material and keeps lectures interesting by telling stories.  Attendance quizzes for easy mark boosting, midterm/exam were not hard at all...  one of the best profs Ive had thus far and I HIGHLY RECOMMEND HIM  =)

He is more interested in making sure you understand the material than in being overly complex.  He assigns long and tedious homework, but makes the exams an easy subset of that (which is ideal for learning, IMHO.  If you did the work, you should do well).  Also added some interesting material that wasn't tested but that was fun to learn.

If you are a student who is not mathematically inclined (like me!), YOU MUST TAKE MACM 316 WITH THIS MAN IF YOU WANT TO PASS. Posts notes online. Exams are very fair (he goes over exactly what kind of questions he will put on the exam during review classes). Homework consists of textbook questions. He gives attendance quizzes, so go to class!
Adam's the best!! He is very clear, concise(most of the time) and makes certain everyone understands the material. The text book was quite bad, but attending lectures helps a whole lot! Kudos!
His notes were useful and he was helpful with homework assignments.  Gave extensions when needed!!
His notes are messy but the exams are easy and straight forward, he is funny sometimes, lectures are not boring, and i think he doesnt like teaching at all!!! o btw he is HOT
No Comments

Nice guy! Don't like teach at all! Notes is "messy". HW never return eventhough u have midterm. No posting solution for HW and midterm. Usually u can't find him when u have problems.He make midterm and exam very easy. So if u want pass the course, u will pass; if learn something, finally is nothing

One of the few profs that can teach in an *intuitive* manner. He doesn't care about the details, but gives you the big picture of formulae/expressions, which I think is nice. For those who like the have fun in interesting math, take his course.

Well, for first he follows the book word by word, which makes you doubt its woth the time going to class. But what I don't like about him is how he seems to choose homework: it's brutal, both the amount and the actual problems, he doesn't bother to solve any himself. But midterm was a piece of cake!
